MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-25-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Grace Bransen Esq., Huguenin Kahn LLP, 916-367-7098, via zoom for plaintiff Jamestown SA LLC
Jessica Beeler Esq., Lewis Brisbois Bisgard & Smith LLP, 415-362-2580 via zoom for defendant Compass California II, Inc.

Ms. Bransen contests the tentative, Ms. Beeler contests the second portion of the tentative.
After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling to the entirety as follows:

DEFENDANTS RICHARD KALUZA, ERIN THOMPSON AND COMPASS CALIFORNIA II, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted as to the paragraphs regarding tort of another. In accordance with the Court's prior ruling, Plaintiff resolved the issue of uncertainty and it is now clear that the tort of another is pled singling out one of the multiple wrongdoers in this action. This is not a proper application of the tort of another doctrine. There are no allegations in the operative complaint that plaintiffs named non-moving co-defendants as defendants in this action as a result of the moving co-defendants negligence, as opposed to the non-moving co-defendants own negligence.
The motion is denied as to the request for emotional distress damages in conjunction with the fraud and breach of fiduciary duty causes of action. Moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that recovery of such damages are not available for intentional torts.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Reporter Rosalia Rodas, CSR 13672, rrcsr27@gmail.com, 562-334-6838. (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-22-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANTS RICHARD KALUZA, ERIN THOMPSON, AND COMPASS CALIFORNIA II, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to (1) clarify which defendants are referenced in paragraphs 18 as "the defendants named herein" (line 11 on page 6) and "the other defendants." (line 12 on page 6). It appears based on the reading of the moving papers and reply that the moving defendants understand "the other defendants" to be defendants other than the moving defendants; while Plaintiff, based on the reading of the opposition, appears to refer to the moving defendants as "the other defendants." The Court notes that in this action that involves multiple defendants, it would be prudent, in order to avoid uncertainty, to name each defendant by name instead of using the generic "the other defendants" or "the defendants named herein" or even "Defendants." (2) Clarify whether the emotional distress damages are sought by both Plaintiffs and whether those damages are sought as part of available recovery under "tort of another" (request appears in conjunction with the tort of another in the operative complaint) or as damages under fraud and breach of fiduciary duty causes of action (as suggested in opposition).

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:(Zoom appearances)
Nick Muscolino, 510.273.8780 for Defendant, Cross-complainant Jonathan Dishotsky.
Susan Shapiro, 415.362.2580 for Defendants, Cross-complainants Richard Kaluza, Erin Thompson and Compass California II, Inc.

Argued by Susan Shapiro. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: CROSS DEFENDANTS RICHARD KALUZA, ERIN THOMPSON and COMPASS CALIFORNIA II, INC.'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is overruled as to the causes of action for indemnity and contribution. Taking the allegations in the complaint as true for the purposes of the demurrer, the applicability of the express indemnity clause presents an issue of fact that cannot be addressed on a demurrer. Demurrer is SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the cause of action for declaratory relief as there is no present controversy between the parties that has not ripened into an action at law. As to the cause of action for tort of another, the demurrer is overruled as the allegations labeled as a cause of action merely support a theory upon which a prayer for attorney's fees is based.
